summary.intent: Fix the ART pipeline dashboard so that runs older than 24 hours can be accessed via direct URL links (e.g., ?run=build-layered-products-ympixd), as they were being filtered out by the default time range filter.
summary.outcome: Identified the root cause in main.py where get_run_detail() applied time filters to all runs, and implemented a fix adding fetch_single_run() and fetch_chain_nodes() helpers to directly fetch PipelineRuns from Kubernetes API when not found in the time-filtered set. Code changes ready to push.

repo: The dashboard runs from the art-dashboard branch of ashwindasr/art-tools fork, not the main repo
Dashboard code lives in art-dashboard/ directory and is built into container images via BuildConfig, not mounted from ConfigMaps
The deployed dashboard is a Python FastAPI/uvicorn app, not the Next.js art-dashboard-ui which is a separate older project
BuildConfig art-dashboard in namespace art-cd pulls from https://github.com/ashwindasr/art-tools.git ref art-dashboard
Pipeline runs have parent/child relationships via labels art.openshift.io/parent-pipelinerun and annotations tekton.dev/tags: triggered

code.finding: get_run_detail() endpoint calls fetch_all_nodes(hours, '', '') which applies time cutoff filter, causing runs older than the time window to return {"error": "not found"}
fetch_all_nodes() calculates cutoff timestamp and skips PipelineRuns with startTime before the cutoff, which is the root cause of the direct-link issue

workflow: Use oc start-build art-dashboard -n art-cd to trigger new builds after pushing code changes
Kubernetes MCP server can inspect pods, deployments, ConfigMaps, and custom resources like Tekton PipelineRuns
PipelineRuns are Tekton custom resources (tekton.dev/v1) accessed via custom_api.get_namespaced_custom_object() and custom_api.list_namespaced_custom_object()

friction: Initial confusion about which dashboard repo/deployment was being discussed (art-dashboard vs art-dashboard-ui)
User repeatedly insisted code was in a ConfigMap despite evidence it was baked into container image
Had to investigate multiple resources (pods, deployments, BuildConfigs, ConfigMaps) to clarify the actual deployment architecture
open_items: Code changes need to be committed and pushed to origin/art-dashboard branch
New build must be triggered on cluster: oc start-build art-dashboard -n art-cd
Should verify fix works after deployment by testing with older run like build-layered-products-ympixd
Optional frontend optimization in templates/index.html to omit hours parameter for direct-linked runs was not implemented
